# Codex Mac Windows Migration Handoff

Codex Mac Windows Migration Handoff is an open-source Codex skill and script toolkit for migrating OpenAI Codex Desktop between macOS and Windows computers.

Repository: https://github.com/CalebYcj/codex-mac-windows-migration-handoff

## Summary

Use this project to package, transfer, restore, and verify Codex Desktop conversations, sessions, memories, skills, plugins, MCP/connectors, generated images, project folders, and path mappings when moving Codex between computers.

Supported migration directions:

- Mac to Windows: real-world migration succeeded.
- Windows to Mac: neutral package layout validated; full Windows-origin restore on Mac still pending.
- Windows to Windows: isolated fake-profile simulation passed.
- Mac to Mac: isolated fake-HOME simulation passed on Intel Mac, x86_64, macOS 12.7.6.

## Primary use cases

- Migrate OpenAI Codex Desktop from Mac to Windows.
- Migrate OpenAI Codex Desktop from Windows to Mac.
- Move Codex Desktop from one Windows computer to another Windows computer.
- Move Codex Desktop from one Mac to another Mac.
- Back up Codex conversations and JSONL sessions.
- Restore Codex memories, skills, plugins, and generated images.
- Hand off a local AI agent workspace to another computer.
- Preserve project context without moving secrets by default.

## Script map

- Package on Mac: `codex-mac-windows-migration-handoff/scripts/create_mac_codex_migration_package.sh`
- Package on Windows: `codex-mac-windows-migration-handoff/scripts/create_windows_codex_migration_package.ps1`
- Restore to Mac: `Restore-Codex-To-Mac.sh` in the generated package, or `scripts/restore_codex_to_mac.sh`
- Restore to Windows: `Restore-Codex-To-Windows.ps1` in the generated package, or `scripts/restore_codex_to_windows.ps1`
- Verify Mac restore: `Verify-Codex-Mac-Restore.sh`
- Verify Windows restore: `Verify-Codex-Windows-Restore.ps1`

## Safety

Standard mode excludes auth tokens, browser cookies, Login Data, Local Storage, `.env` files, private keys, sockets, `.git`, `node_modules`, virtual environments, and runtime caches. The target computer should log in again manually.

Do not rewrite old Codex session JSONL files in bulk. Preserve old paths in history and reopen matching project folders on the target computer.

## Validation

See validation status for exact tested scope, caveats, and verifier counts:

- https://github.com/CalebYcj/codex-mac-windows-migration-handoff/blob/main/docs/validation-status.md
